steps.title: Identify the sides relative to angle x°
steps.content: The right angle is marked at the bottom-left corner. Angle $x°$ is at the top-left vertex. For this angle, the adjacent side (the side touching $x°$ that is not the hypotenuse) is the vertical leg with length 11. The hypotenuse (opposite the right angle) is labeled 28.

steps.title: Apply the cosine ratio
steps.content:
Cosine is defined as the ratio of the adjacent side to the hypotenuse:

$$\cos(x°) = \frac{\text{adjacent}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{11}{28}$$

Calculating the decimal value: $\frac{11}{28} = 0.3928$.
